task_completion.description: Create a new encounter and document HPI, ROS, and Physical Exam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the NOSH EHR showing a new encounter for Margaret Thompson. The HPI, Review of Systems (ROS), and Physical Exam (PE) sections should contain entered text or checked boxes for multiple systems.

task_completion.description: Implement medication recommendations from the consultation letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the patient's medication list or encounter plan, Metformin should be updated to 1000mg BID (or the 500mg discontinued and 1000mg added), and Clopidogrel 75mg daily should be added as a new active medication.

task_completion.description: Identify and resolve the unsafe drug interaction while retaining safe medications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Naproxen must be marked as discontinued, inactive, or removed from the active medications list (due to interaction with Clopidogrel). Omeprazole must remain on the active medications list.

task_completion.description: Update the problem list with correct diagnoses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient's problem list or assessment section shows 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus' (or E11.9) and 'Osteoarthritis' (or M19.90). 'Type 1 Diabetes' should be removed or marked inactive.

task_completion.description: Order recommended labs and place an Ophthalmology referral.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The orders or plan section shows lab orders for HbA1c, Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP), and Lipid Panel. A referral to Ophthalmology (for diabetic retinopathy screening) should also be visible.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the NOSH EHR graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the NOSH web interface being used, with forms filled out, buttons clicked, and menus navigated, rather than terminal commands or direct database modifications.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the consultation letter.
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the consultation_letter.txt file open in a text editor or viewer on the desktop.

integrity.description: The agent did not mass-discontinue medications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other safe medications like Lisinopril and Atorvastatin should still be visible in the active medications list. Only Naproxen (and the old dose of Metformin) should be discontinued.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Margaret Thompson (DOB: 1958-04-22). The consultation letter recommends increasing Metformin to 1000mg BID and starting Clopidogrel 75mg daily. The patient's seeded active medications include Naproxen 500mg and Omeprazole 20mg. The critical hidden challenge is that Naproxen (an NSAID) combined with Clopidogrel (an antiplatelet) creates a major GI bleeding risk. The agent must independently discover this interaction and discontinue Naproxen. Omeprazole is gastroprotective and should be retained. The problem list initially has an incorrect 'Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (E10.9)' which must be corrected to Type 2 (E11.9). Osteoarthritis (M19.90) must be added. Labs to order: HbA1c, CMP, Lipid Panel. Referral: Ophthalmology.
